records.ety: [OF. denzein, deinzein, prop., one living (a city or country); opposed to forain foreign, and fr. denz within, F. dans, fr. L. de intus, prop., from within, intus being from in in. See In, and cf. Foreign.]

quotes: Denizens of their own free, independent state.

defs: One who is admitted by favor to all or a part of the rights of citizenship, where he did not possess them by birth; an adopted or naturalized citizen.
One admitted to residence in a foreign country.
quotes: Ye gods, Natives, or denizens, of blest abodes.

defs: To constitute (one) a denizen; to admit to residence, with certain rights and privileges.
quotes: As soon as denizened, they domineer.

defs: To provide with denizens; to populate with adopted or naturalized occupants.
quotes: There [islets] were at once denizened by various weeds.
